annotations.label: Projective plane (informal)

anchor.snippet: A projective plane can be thought of as an ordinary plane equipped with additional

annotations.note: Mathlib defines `Configuration.ProjectivePlane P L` axiomatically as a nondegenerate configuration with `HasPoints`, `HasLines`, and a three-points-in-general-position witness.

annotations.label: Projective plane (incidence structure)

anchor.snippet: consisting of a set of points

annotations.note: The class `Configuration.ProjectivePlane` packages exactly the incidence axioms (any two points on a unique line, any two lines meet, three-point configuration).

annotations.label: Order of a projective plane

anchor.snippet: is called order of the plane

annotations.note: `Configuration.ProjectivePlane.order P L` is defined as `lineCount L p - 1` for the chosen configuration point, with `lineCount_eq` showing it is one less than the number of lines through any point.

annotations.label: Extended Euclidean plane

anchor.snippet: To turn the ordinary Euclidean plane into a projective plane, proceed as follows

annotations.note: No formal construction of the projective completion of the Euclidean plane (with points-at-infinity / line-at-infinity) was found.

annotations.label: Projective Moulton plane

anchor.snippet: The points of the Moulton plane are the points of the Euclidean plane

annotations.note: Grep for `Moulton` returns no Mathlib match; the Moulton plane is not formalized.

annotations.label: Projective plane of order three

anchor.snippet: This example has just thirteen points and thirteen lines

annotations.note: PG(2, ZMod 3) arises by specializing the general `Projectivization K (Fin 3 → K)` projective-plane instance to `K = ZMod 3`, but no dedicated decl for the thirteen-point plane exists.

annotations.label: Projective plane over a division ring PG(2,K)

anchor.snippet: has a set of points consisting of all the 1-dimensional subspaces

annotations.note: Mathlib provides `ProjectivePlane (ℙ K (Fin 3 → K)) (ℙ K (Fin 3 → K))` only for `K` a field (with `DecidableEq`), not for a general division ring.

annotations.label: Homogeneous coordinates

anchor.snippet: are called homogeneous coordinates

annotations.note: `Projectivization K V` is the quotient of nonzero vectors by scalar action — exactly homogeneous coordinates — but the name `homogeneous coordinates` is not used.

annotations.label: Real projective plane RP^2

anchor.snippet: The real projective plane RP 2 arises when K is taken to be the real numbers

annotations.note: `Projectivization ℝ (Fin 3 → ℝ)` realizes RP² and inherits the ProjectivePlane instance, but no dedicated `RP^2` abbreviation/decl exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Complex projective plane CP^2

anchor.snippet: The complex projective plane CP 2 arises when K is taken to be the complex numbers

annotations.note: Same as RP²: `Projectivization ℂ (Fin 3 → ℂ)` instantiates CP² as a projective plane, but Mathlib has no dedicated `CP^2` decl.

annotations.label: Quaternionic projective plane HP^2

anchor.snippet: The quaternionic projective plane HP 2 is also of independent interest

annotations.note: Mathlib's `Configuration.ofField` projective-plane instance requires a field, so `Projectivization` over the (non-commutative) quaternions is not equipped as a projective plane.

annotations.label: Field planes from finite fields

anchor.snippet: Taking K to be the finite field of q

annotations.note: The PG(2, F_q) instance is obtained as a specialization of Mathlib's general `Projectivization K (Fin 3 → K)` projective-plane instance for any field K.

annotations.label: Wedderburn's theorem (finite division ring is a field)

anchor.snippet: By Wedderburn's Theorem

annotations.note: `littleWedderburn` is an instance turning every `[DivisionRing D] [Finite D]` into a `Field D`.

annotations.label: Fano plane

anchor.snippet: The Fano plane is the projective plane arising from the field of two elements

annotations.note: Grep for `Fano` returns no match; the Fano plane is only obtainable as the special case `K = ZMod 2` of the PG(2,K) instance.

annotations.label: Desargues' theorem characterization of vector-space planes

anchor.snippet: The theorem of Desargues is universally valid in a projective plane if and only if the plane can be constructed

annotations.note: No occurrences of `Desargues` in Mathlib; neither Desargues' theorem nor the characterization of Desarguesian planes is formalized.

annotations.label: Non-Desarguesian plane

anchor.snippet: The projective planes that can not be constructed in the preceding manner are called non-Desarguesian planes

annotations.note: No Mathlib match for non-Desarguesian planes.

annotations.label: Subplane

anchor.snippet: A subplane of a projective plane

annotations.note: Grep for `subplane`/`Subplane` returns no matches in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Bruck's theorem on subplane orders

anchor.snippet: Let Π be a finite projective plane of order N with a proper subplane

annotations.note: No notion of subplane in Mathlib, so Bruck's theorem cannot be stated.

annotations.label: Baer subplane

anchor.snippet: is a Baer subplane if every line in

annotations.note: No `Baer` subplane in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Existence of Baer subplanes in finite Desarguesian planes

anchor.snippet: admits Baer subplanes (all necessarily Desarguesian) if and only if

annotations.note: Baer subplane existence is not formalized.

annotations.label: Fano subplane

anchor.snippet: A Fano subplane is a subplane isomorphic to PG(2, 2)

annotations.note: No notion of Fano subplane in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Fano subplanes in PG(2,q)

anchor.snippet: Fano subplanes exist if and only if q is even

annotations.note: Not formalized.

annotations.label: Gleason's theorem on Fano subplanes

anchor.snippet: If every quadrangle in a finite projective plane has collinear diagonal points

annotations.note: Gleason's theorem on Fano subplanes is absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: Affine plane

anchor.snippet: More formally an affine plane consists of a set of lines and a set of points

annotations.note: Mathlib has `AffineSpace`/`AffineMap` but no axiomatic incidence-geometric `AffinePlane` class.

annotations.label: Order of a finite affine plane

anchor.snippet: The order of a finite affine plane is the number of points on any of its lines

annotations.note: Absent because the underlying affine-plane definition is missing.

annotations.label: Existence equivalence of affine and projective planes of order N

anchor.snippet: There is a projective plane of order N if and only if there is an affine plane of order N

annotations.note: Requires the missing AffinePlane axiomatic side; not formalized.

annotations.label: Embedding of affine plane into K P^2

anchor.section: Construction of projective planes from affine planes
anchor.snippet: The affine plane K 2 over K embeds into K P 2

annotations.note: No formal embedding `K^2 ↪ ℙ K (Fin 3 → K)` recorded as an incidence-geometric construction.

annotations.label: Planar ternary ring

anchor.snippet: One can construct a coordinate "ring"—a so-called planar ternary ring

annotations.note: Grep for `PlanarTernaryRing`/`ternaryRing` returns no matches.

annotations.label: Cayley plane (octonionic projective plane)

anchor.snippet: The Cayley plane

annotations.note: The Cayley plane (OP²) is not in Mathlib; octonions exist (`Octonion`) but no associated projective plane.

annotations.label: Pappian plane

anchor.snippet: A projective plane satisfying Pappus's theorem universally is called a Pappian plane

annotations.note: Neither Pappus' theorem in projective planes nor Pappian planes are formalized.

annotations.label: Moufang plane

anchor.snippet: division algebras like the octonions correspond to Moufang planes

annotations.note: No `Moufang` plane in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Construction of finite projective plane from PTR

anchor.snippet: To describe a finite projective plane of order N

annotations.note: Not formalized — PTR construction is absent.

annotations.label: Degenerate plane

anchor.snippet: Degenerate planes do not fulfill the third condition in the definition of a projective plane

annotations.note: Mathlib's `Configuration.Nondegenerate` class captures the negation, but there is no dedicated `Degenerate plane` decl enumerating the seven classes.

annotations.label: Collineation

anchor.snippet: A collineation of a projective plane is a bijective map of the plane to itself

annotations.note: No `Collineation` decl in Mathlib for projective planes.

annotations.label: Fixed point and fixed line of a collineation

anchor.snippet: is called a fixed point of

annotations.note: Without `Collineation`, fixed-point/fixed-line concepts for collineations are unformalized.

annotations.label: Homography

anchor.snippet: A homography (or projective transformation ) of PG(2, K ) is a collineation of this type

annotations.note: Grep finds no `Homography` in Mathlib (the projective-linear group is the closest, but it is not formalized as a homography of PG(2,K)).

annotations.label: Automorphic collineation

anchor.snippet: these are called automorphic collineations

annotations.note: Not formalized.

annotations.label: Fundamental theorem of projective geometry

anchor.snippet: The fundamental theorem of projective geometry says that all the collineations

annotations.note: No formal statement of the fundamental theorem of projective geometry.

annotations.label: Plane dual structure

anchor.snippet: we obtain the dual structure

annotations.note: `Configuration.Dual` provides the type synonym swapping points and lines, with membership defined as `Function.swap`.

annotations.label: Plane dual statement

anchor.snippet: is called the plane dual statement of the first

annotations.note: There is no meta-level operation in Mathlib for forming the syntactic dual of a statement.

annotations.label: Dual plane is a projective plane

anchor.snippet: Dualizing this theorem and the first two axioms in the definition of a projective plane shows that the plane dual structure

annotations.note: Mathlib provides `instance : ProjectivePlane (Dual L) (Dual P)`, witnessing that the dual of a projective plane is a projective plane.

annotations.label: Self-dual plane

anchor.snippet: is called self-dual

annotations.note: No `SelfDual` projective plane definition in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Principle of plane duality

anchor.snippet: The Principle of plane duality says that dualizing any theorem in a self-dual projective plane

annotations.note: The metatheorem isn't formalized; only the structural duality instance exists.

annotations.label: Duality and correlation

anchor.snippet: A duality is a map from a projective plane

annotations.note: No `Duality`/`Correlation` map between a projective plane and its dual is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Reciprocity

anchor.snippet: a duality is called a reciprocity

annotations.note: `Reciprocity` in the projective-plane sense is not in Mathlib (the matches refer to quadratic reciprocity).

annotations.label: Polarity

anchor.snippet: A correlation of order two (an involution ) is called a polarity

annotations.note: No projective-geometric `Polarity` in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Counting in finite projective planes

anchor.snippet: It can be shown that a projective plane has the same number of lines as it has points

annotations.note: `card_points_eq_card_lines`, `card_points`, `card_lines`, `lineCount_eq`, and `pointCount_eq` together formalize the standard `N² + N + 1` counting in finite projective planes.

annotations.label: Bruck–Ryser–Chowla theorem

anchor.snippet: the Bruck–Ryser–Chowla theorem that if the order N is congruent to 1 or 2 mod 4

annotations.note: Bruck–Ryser–Chowla is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Projective plane as Steiner system

anchor.snippet: A projective plane of order N is a Steiner

annotations.note: Mathlib has no `SteinerSystem`/`SteinerTripleSystem` definition.

annotations.label: MOLS and projective planes

anchor.snippet: The number of mutually orthogonal Latin squares of order N is at most

annotations.note: No MOLS / Latin-square infrastructure in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Higher-dimensional projective spaces are Desarguesian

anchor.section: Projective planes in higher-dimensional projective spaces
anchor.snippet: the extra degrees of freedom permit Desargues' theorem to be proved geometrically

annotations.note: Desargues' theorem itself isn't formalized, so this consequence isn't either.

annotations.label: Non-embeddability of non-Desarguesian planes

anchor.section: Projective planes in higher-dimensional projective spaces
anchor.snippet: It can be shown that if Desargues' theorem holds in a projective space of dimension greater than two

annotations.note: Depends on Desargues' theorem, which is absent from Mathlib.
